scientific_integrity.detail: Citation verification rule requires real, verifiable references with DOI when formal citations are included

code_usability.detail: No code generated; Category 1 evidence mapping only

functional_suitability.note: Description is accurate but does not include natural trigger phrases for the wide range of mechanism mapping scenarios

reliability.note: Literature verification requirement is strong; gap: no defined behavior when no verifiable citations are available at all

performance_context.note: 267 lines; eleven reference modules appropriately organized

agent_usability.note: Output requirement section mixes structural guidance with hard rules in a way that may cause agents to miss the mandatory K section for verified citations

agent_specific.note: Nine-step execution logic is thorough; downstream routing integration is a composability strength

inputs.label: Disease mechanism map for sepsis immune paralysis

assertions.text: Mechanism evidence organized into layered chains (molecule → pathway → cell → tissue → phenotype)
Direct vs indirect vs inference labels applied to each key link
Human evidence distinguished from animal and cell-line evidence
Weak links identified without premature conversion to formal research gaps
Verified citations with DOI where available; unverifiable citations not presented as formal support

inputs.label: Ferroptosis in diabetic nephropathy — pathway-centered mechanism map

assertions.text: Dominant mechanism axes prioritized over exhaustive pathway list
Cell types and tissue compartments linked to phenotype in Section D/E
Evidence strength and chain completeness labeled per segment
Mechanism hypothesis entry points suggested without overclaiming causality
Downstream routing recommendation present

inputs.label: HCC immunosuppressive microenvironment — cell-type-centered mechanism map

assertions.text: Cell-state evidence distinguished from bulk-population evidence
Indirect associations not presented as completed mechanism chains
Key evidence chain table present with mechanism axes summarized
No generic pathway dump produced
Citation DOIs verified where included or absence explicitly noted

inputs.label: Very early-stage mechanism with only cell-line evidence and no human data
inputs.note: Evidence density very low; output appropriately constrained but could provide more structure

assertions.text: Cell-line-only evidence correctly not equated with human disease validation
Chain completeness labeled as 'incomplete' for all cross-layer connections
Speculative segments clearly labeled as speculative
Hypothesis entry points suggested conservatively
When no verifiable formal citations available, this is stated explicitly rather than omitting Section K entirely
assertions.note: Section K was omitted entirely rather than producing a 'No verified formal citations available for this emerging topic' statement

inputs.label: Lupus nephritis tubulointerstitial injury — multi-compartment mechanism map

assertions.text: Multiple tissue compartments mapped with separate evidence chains
Contradictory mechanism reports represented directly
Evidence strength and chain completeness labeled per compartment
Map does not confuse model-system evidence with human disease closure
Section K verified citations present with appropriate caveats

key_strengths: Citation verification requirement with mandatory DOI and explicit unverifiability statements is one of the strongest integrity safeguards in this collection
Five-layer evidence chain structure (molecule → pathway → cell → tissue → phenotype) provides a systematic and reproducible mapping framework
Direct vs indirect vs inference labeling at the individual link level prevents mechanism chain overclaiming
Downstream routing integration makes this skill a strong natural upstream tool in the research workflow

recommendations.title: Section K omitted entirely when no verifiable citations are available
recommendations.problem: When no verified formal citations can be included for an emerging/sparse topic, Section K is sometimes omitted rather than producing an explicit statement that no verified formal citations are currently available.
recommendations.root_cause: Output structure guidance says Section K is for 'when citations are included' but does not mandate a 'No verified citations' placeholder when the topic is evidence-sparse.
recommendations.fix: Update Section K guidance: if no formal verified citations can be produced, output the section with text: 'No verified formal citations were identified for this topic at the required confidence level. Key mechanism claims are based on training knowledge and should be verified with current PubMed/Embase search.'

recommendations.title: Output requirement section mixes structural guidance with hard rules — risk of Section K being skipped
recommendations.problem: Section K for verified citations is in 'Output Requirements' but labeled as conditional ('when citations are included'), making it easier for agents to skip when citations are uncertain.
recommendations.root_cause: Optional framing reduces enforcement of citation integrity in sparse evidence contexts.
recommendations.fix: Make Section K mandatory for all outputs: either include verified citations OR include an explicit statement that no verified citations are available. Remove the conditional 'when citations are included' language.

recommendations.title: Description lacks natural trigger phrases for diverse mapping scenarios
recommendations.problem: Description focuses on formal structure ('layered mechanism evidence chains') but omits the natural user phrases that would trigger this skill.
recommendations.root_cause: Description written for content accuracy rather than triggering optimization.
recommendations.fix: Add trigger phrases: 'mechanism evidence map', 'disease pathway overview', 'mechanism architecture before study design', 'how this disease works', 'evidence chain for this pathway'.
